metadata.role_title: Senior Data Scientist - Generative AI / AI Engineer - LLMOps (2-5 yrs)
metadata.match_reason: Fortune 500 AI partner with public GenAI/LLM offerings (LLM Studio on NVIDIA, Kyron AI); active GenAI Data Scientist + AI Engineer LLMOps roles in Bengaluru; Python/PyTorch/LLM/RAG/AWS stack.
metadata.notes: 57+ India roles on Workday;  ~66 open per Glassdoor; multiple Bengaluru GenAI/LLM postings.

research_summary: Fractal Analytics is a publicly listed (NSE) Fortune 500 AI partner with 5K-10K employees building enterprise GenAI and agentic AI products. The Bengaluru team is actively hiring Data Scientists for GenAI and Agentic Systems (SR-42113, posted 8 days ago). Key recent signals: LLM Studio at NVIDIA GTC March 2026, Cogentiq agentic platform July 2025, AWS/Microsoft AI partnerships. The stack (Python, LangChain, RAG, vector DBs, fine-tuning, FastAPI) maps exactly to Amit's production work. 6 contacts: 2 Tier 1 (AI Growth Lead, Lead Data Scientist), 2 Tier 3 (Senior Data Scientists), 2 Tier 4 (TA Managers for analytics hiring).
jd_full_text:
Data Scientist / Sr. Data Scientist - GenAI & Agentic Systems

About Fractal
Fractal is one of the most prominent players in the Artificial Intelligence space. Fractal's mission is to power every human decision in the enterprise. Fractal provides Al to Fortune 500 companies such as Pfizer, Unilever, Walmart, Microsoft, General Mills, etc. to transform their business and power their decisions.

Role Overview
As a Data Scientist / Sr. Data Scientist with a focus on Generative AI and Agentic Systems, you will design and build advanced AI solutions using LLMs, RAG pipelines, and multi-agent frameworks for enterprise clients.

Key Responsibilities
- Architect and deploy cutting-edge LLM-based solutions and agentic workflows
- Build RAG pipelines with knowledge retrieval (dense/sparse retrieval techniques)
- Own full lifecycle: problem scoping, model design, deployment, post-production optimization
- Develop reusable frameworks, pipelines, and prompt libraries for GenAI
- Work with cross-functional teams to integrate solutions into core client systems
- Apply strong analytical and problem-solving skills to enterprise data challenges

Required Skills
- ML/Deep Learning fundamentals (Transformers, LSTMs, RNNs, supervised/unsupervised)
- GenAI expertise: GPT-4, Claude, Llama; fine-tuning and optimization (PEFT, RLHF)
- NLP: text preprocessing, embeddings, sentiment analysis, classification, summarization, Q&A, Document AI
- RAG pipelines with knowledge retrieval (dense/sparse)
- Python, PyTorch, Hugging Face libraries
- LangChain, LlamaIndex, vector databases (ChromaDB, PGVector, Elasticsearch, Milvus)
- Azure OpenAI, Google Vertex AI, AWS Bedrock
- Advanced prompt engineering: COT, TOT, ReAct, agents
- Cloud platforms: Azure, AWS, GCP (certification preferred)
- Application development: Python, Docker, FastAPI/Django/Flask, Git
- Evaluation metrics and benchmarking for generative models

Location: Bengaluru, India
Seniority: 2-5 years experience
tailoring_notes: Summary rewritten to lead with RAG + agentic AI for enterprise clients -- JD explicitly asks for RAG pipelines and agentic workflows for Fortune 500 clients. Skills reordered: LLMs and Techniques first (RAG, Agentic AI per JD), Languages 2nd, Vector DBs 3rd, Infrastructure 4th; Hardware/IoT dropped. IIT Delhi trimmed to 1 bullet. Projects: WeepScope replaced with SAP Analytics Bot (NLP/production system depth).

contacts.research_hook: Describes his work as helping Fortune 500s implement Agentic AI and Gen AI at scale. Active in Fractal's LLM Studio rollout at NVIDIA GTC 2026 and Cogentiq enterprise deployments -- exactly the production-scale enterprise AI problem Amit has been solving.
contacts.subject: production RAG at Fortune 500 scale
contacts.content:
Hi Raghav,

Went through the LLM Studio coverage from NVIDIA GTC last month. The AutoLLM plus LLMOps split addresses something I've run into building production RAG: the gap between a benchmark-passing system and one that holds at enterprise query volume.

I've spent two years on that gap. At Silver Touch I rebuilt RAG infrastructure from FAISS to Milvus, 45% to 95% MRR, after testing six embedding models on production data. At Vivansh I built the same kind of system at 500+ concurrent users with 2-stage retrieval and 250+ per-query access control checks.

The Fortune 500 AI rollouts you work on are exactly where retrieval architecture choices compound. If the Bengaluru team is hiring on the AI engineering side, I'd appreciate 15 minutes to hear how you think about the accuracy problem at that scale. Tuesday or Thursday next week works.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | github.com/amit-gohel | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: production RAG at Fortune 500 scale
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Raghav,

Following up in case this got buried. One thing I left out: the multi-tenant access control layer at Vivansh made the problem harder than standard RAG. Permission filtering and retrieval accuracy pull against each other, and a 2-index architecture was the only solution that held at 500+ concurrent users.

If timing is not right, no problem.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: production RAG at Fortune 500 scale
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Raghav,

Last note from me. If Fractal's Bengaluru team is not actively looking right now, totally understood. Would appreciate being on your radar when it comes up.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat

contacts.research_hook: Posted about RAG production tradeoffs: retrieval quality vs latency is the core challenge; reranking + larger candidate sets + richer context each add latency. Also posted about fine-tuning with LoRA adapters (50K curated reviews, HuggingFace PEFT on Azure GPU) to handle domain-specific vocabulary gaps in smaller models -- identical problems to what Amit solved at Silver Touch and Vivansh.
contacts.subject: your RAG latency post last month
contacts.content:
Hi Aiswarya,

Read your post about production RAG tradeoffs. The tension between retrieval quality and latency (reranking improves accuracy but each added step costs latency) is exactly the problem I spent a year working through at Silver Touch.

My solution there: tested six embedding models on production data before landing on BGE-M3 plus a reranker, then layered intent classification on top to bring latency back down. At Vivansh I built 2-stage retrieval across 10,000+ chunks at 500+ concurrent users, with GPT-4o handling query rewriting. Your point about domain vocabulary gaps in smaller models also tracked with what I saw in production. A PEFT-fine-tuned model is often the right call when a frontier model is too expensive per query.

I've been tracking what Fractal's Bengaluru team is building. If there's a fit on the AI engineering side, I'd appreciate 15 minutes. Tuesday or Thursday works.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | github.com/amit-gohel | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: your RAG latency post last month
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Aiswarya,

Following up in case this got lost. One detail I left out: at Silver Touch the FAISS to Milvus migration was the easy part. Getting the embedding evaluation to reflect production query distributions rather than benchmark scores was harder, and that is where the six-model test on real data mattered.

If timing is not right, no worries.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: your RAG latency post last month
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Aiswarya,

Last note from me. If the Bengaluru team is not actively hiring right now, totally understood. Happy to stay on your radar.

Good luck with whatever you are building on the agentic side.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat

contacts.research_hook: Senior Data Scientist focused on NLP, Generative AI, and MLOps -- 5K followers on LinkedIn, active poster on enterprise AI production problems. MLOps certification signals deep deployment focus, matching Amit's vLLM and production inference background.
contacts.subject: NLP and RAG deployment at Fractal
contacts.content:
Hi Debasis,

Came across your LinkedIn while researching Fractal's Bengaluru GenAI team. Your background combining NLP and GenAI with MLOps certification caught my eye, specifically the production deployment side.

At Silver Touch I rebuilt a 50,000-query-per-month RAG chatbot from FAISS to Milvus, improving MRR from 45% to 95% after testing six embedding models. At Vivansh I added 2-stage retrieval with permission filtering and vLLM-served inference, reaching 500+ concurrent users. The MLOps layer (monitoring retrieval quality live, tuning reranker thresholds in production) was where a lot of the reliability work happened.

If Fractal's Bengaluru team is hiring for GenAI or agentic AI roles, would you know the right person to reach out to?

Amit
amitgohel.dev | github.com/amit-gohel | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat

contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Debasis,

Following up in case this got buried. One thing I left out: the vLLM migration at Silver Touch cut model serving overhead significantly compared to the original in-code Llama-2-7b setup. That kind of inference infrastructure work is something I want to keep building on.

Amit

contacts.research_hook: Profile tagline: 'Generative AI | Agentic frameworks | test and control SME' -- working on evaluation and testing methodology for agentic AI at Fractal. Connects to Amit's Pydantic-validated intermediate schemas which are essentially a reliability/test layer for LLM output pipelines.
contacts.subject: agentic AI reliability and test control
contacts.content:
Hi Kratika,

Your focus on agentic frameworks and test and control stood out while I was looking into Fractal's Bengaluru engineering team.

At Vivansh I built a multi-model orchestration pipeline and found that a Pydantic-validated intermediate schema was what made it production-stable. GPT-4o and Claude both failed to reliably generate the platform's native JSON format without that validation layer. That reliability design sits at the intersection of agentic pipeline architecture and production testing, which is exactly what your tagline points at.

I've been tracking what Fractal is building with Cogentiq. If the Bengaluru team is hiring, would you know the right person to connect with?

Amit
amitgohel.dev | github.com/amit-gohel | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: agentic AI reliability and test control
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Kratika,

Following up in case this got lost. The piece I think is underrated in agentic systems: most failures are not model failures. They are schema or state propagation failures between agent steps. That is the problem the Pydantic layer solved in production, and why I think test and control deserves its own architectural layer.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: agentic AI reliability and test control
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Kratika,

Last note from me. If the team is not actively hiring right now, no problem. Would still appreciate being on your radar.

Amit

contacts.subject: AI engineer background for Fractal Bengaluru
contacts.content:
Hi Sakthi,

Reaching out because I think my background is a clean match for what Fractal hires for on the AI side.

Two years of production Python AI: RAG chatbots at 95% MRR handling 50,000 queries per month, agentic LangChain pipelines on multi-tenant platforms, multi-model LLM orchestration across GPT-4o, Anthropic Claude, and Gemini 2.5 Pro, and on-premise document processing for sensitive data. Current role at Vivansh Infotech in Ahmedabad; prior research at IIT Delhi. Microsoft Azure AI Engineer Associate certified.

Resume and live projects: amitgohel.dev

If there is an open GenAI or agentic AI role on the Bengaluru team I should look at, I would appreciate a pointer.

Thanks,
Amit
